page.content: In the first trial of 2008, the plant was cabbage, with a date of transplanting on 15 4 2008, using the Bt treatment of Bt kurstaki on 15 5 2008, and the date of pest release on 27 5 2008, with a quantity per plant of approximately 160 eggs, followed by a date of virus application on 2 6 2008, with the larval stage being late 1st-instar, a concentration of NPV at 106 OBs/mL, a concentration of GVPs of 0 or 10 μg/mL, and a date of larval collection on 6 6 2008, with the larval stage being late 2nd.

on 15 5 2008, and the date of pest release on 27 5 2008, with a quantity per plant of approximately 160 eggs, followed by a date

concentration of GVPs of 0 or 10 μg/mL, and a date of larval collection on 6 6 2008, with the larval stage being late 2nd.

page.content: In the second trial of 2008, the plant remained cabbage, with a date of transplanting on 23 5 2008, no Bt treatment was applied, and the date of pest release was between 29 6–1 7 2008, with a quantity per plant of approximately 320 eggs, followed by a date of virus application on 7 7 2008, with the larval stage being 2nd to 3rd-instars, a concentration of NPV at 105 OBs/mL, a concentration of GVPs of 0 or 10 μg/mL, and a date of larval collection on 11 7 2008, with the larval stage being 4th. In the first trial of 2009, the plant was cabbage, with a date of transplanting on 20 4 2009, no Bt treatment, and the date of pest release on 29–30 5 2009, with a quantity per plant of 300–400 eggs, followed by a date of virus application on 4 6 2009, with the larval stage being 2nd-instar, a concentration of NPV at 105 OBs/mL, a concentration of GVPs of 5 or 10 μg/mL, and a date of larval collection on 8 6 2009, with the larval stage being late 2nd to mid-3rd. In the second trial of 2009, the plant was broccoli, with a date of transplanting on 12 8 2009, utilizing Bt kurstaki on 3 9 2009, and the date of pest release on 11–12 9 2009, with a quantity per plant of 300–400 eggs or larvae, followed by a date of virus application on 19 9 2009, with the larval stage being 2nd-instar, a concentration of NPV at 105 OBs/mL, a concentration of GVPs of 5 or 10 μg/mL, and a date of larval collection on 23 9 2009, with the larval stage being 3rd to 4th.
